title: tephroseris tundricola subsp. lindstroemii
intro: Tephroseris tundricola subsp. lindstroemii, also known as Lindstroem's tephroseris, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of flowering plant that is native to the Arctic regions of Europe and Asia, and is often found in rocky and mountainous areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, and is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Lindstroem's tephroseris typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and roots, which are said to provide health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
